titleStmt.title: SII 18.27: original edition by N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 27. (B.K. No. 178 of 1933-34.) KĀRJŌḶ, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.hi: Slab called the Kapilavva set up in a field three miles north of the village.

summary.p: This inscription referring itself to the reign of Kanharadēva, is dated in Śaka 879, Piṅgaḷa, Ās11vayuja, śu. Pañchami, Thursday. The details correspond to A.D. 957, September 1, which was a Tuesday and not Thursday, as stated in the record. It may be noted, however, that ba. 5 of the same month, (i.e., September 17) was a Thursday. The king is stated to be ruling the kingdom from Mēlpāṭi.
It states that when his feudatory Tailapayya, was governing the nāḍu, a grant of land was made to a tank called Saṁbayyana-keṟe. The name of the donor is lost.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
namaḥ sivāya tridasārccitāya vinaṣṭadō
guṇārṇṇavāya vimukta pratibōdha
svasti sakanṛpakāḷātītasaṁvatsarasataṁ
gaḷeṇṭunūṟaEḻpattoṁbhattaneya piṁgaḷa saṁ
vatsaraṁ pravarttise tadvariṣābhyantarada Āśvayu
ja suddha pañcami bṛhaspativāra śrīkanharadēvaṁ
mēlpāṭiyoḷ rājyaṁgeyyuttire tatpādapadmōpajī
vi tailapayyannāḍanāḷuttuvirēḷukōṭeya
ddayyana dhammaṁ dhātāraṁ saṁbayyana keṟe
ge biṭṭa keyyi mattarppattu Idanaḻidukoṇḍā
taṁ prayāge bāṇarāsiyoḷ kavileyuṁ pā
rvvaru liṁgamuṁ tavaśyarumanaḻida pāta
kaṁ . ślō . bahubhirvvasudhā bhutvā rāja
bhissantarājabhiryasya yasya yadā bhūmi
tasya tasya tathā phalaṁ . ya dataṁ vā
yō harēti vasundharī ṣaṣṭiṁ variṣasahaśrā
ṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē krimi .
pracāḷitaravindumaṇḍaḷam Ākuñcatē sēṣamurvvarājaḷadhi
tpaḷitasya saṁbhōrubhaya
namaḥ śivāya namaḥ śivāyaThe letters
Digital edition of SII 18.27 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
